# influxql

```go
import "github.com/influxdata/influxql"
```

Package influxql implements a parser for the InfluxDB query language.

InfluxQL is a DML and DDL language for the InfluxDB time series database. It provides the ability to query for aggregate statistics as well as create and configure the InfluxDB server.

See https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/latest/query_language/ for a reference on using InfluxQL.

## Constants

<a name="MinTime"></a>

```go
const (
    // MinTime is the minumum time that can be represented.
    //
    // 1677-09-21 00:12:43.145224194 +0000 UTC
    //
    // The two lowest minimum integers are used as sentinel values.  The
    // minimum value needs to be used as a value lower than any other value for
    // comparisons and another separate value is needed to act as a sentinel
    // default value that is unusable by the user, but usable internally.
    // Because these two values need to be used for a special purpose, we do
    // not allow users to write points at these two times.
    MinTime = int64(math.MinInt64) + 2

    // MaxTime is the maximum time that can be represented.
    //
    // 2262-04-11 23:47:16.854775806 +0000 UTC
    //
    // The highest time represented by a nanosecond needs to be used for an
    // exclusive range in the shard group, so the maximum time needs to be one
    // less than the possible maximum number of nanoseconds representable by an
    // int64 so that we don't lose a point at that one time.
    MaxTime = int64(math.MaxInt64) - 1
)
```

<a name="DateFormat"></a>

```go
const (
    // DateFormat represents the format for date literals.
    DateFormat = "2006-01-02"

    // DateTimeFormat represents the format for date time literals.
    DateTimeFormat = "2006-01-02 15:04:05.999999"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="ErrInvalidDuration"></a>ErrInvalidDuration is returned when parsing a malformed duration.

```go
var ErrInvalidDuration = errors.New("invalid duration")
```

<a name="ErrInvalidTime"></a>

```go
var (
    // ErrInvalidTime is returned when the timestamp string used to
    // compare against time field is invalid.
    ErrInvalidTime = errors.New("invalid timestamp string")
)
```

<a name="Language"></a>

```go
var Language = &ParseTree{}
```

<a name="BinaryExprName"></a>

## func [BinaryExprName](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4010>)

```go
func BinaryExprName(expr *BinaryExpr) string
```

BinaryExprName returns the name of a binary expression by concatenating the variables in the binary expression with underscores.

<a name="ConditionExpr"></a>

## func [ConditionExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5987>)

```go
func ConditionExpr(cond Expr, valuer Valuer) (Expr, TimeRange, error)
```

ConditionExpr extracts the time range and the condition from an expression. We only support simple time ranges that are constrained with AND and are not nested. This throws an error when we encounter a time condition that is combined with OR to prevent returning unexpected results that we do not support.

ContainsVarRef returns true if expr is a VarRef or contains one.


<a name="Sanitize"></a>

## func [Sanitize](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/sanitize.go#L22>)

```go
func Sanitize(query string) string
```

Sanitize attempts to sanitize passwords out of a raw query. It looks for patterns that may be related to the SET PASSWORD and CREATE USER statements and will redact the password that should be there. It will attempt to redact information from common invalid queries too, but it's not guaranteed to succeed on improper queries.

This function works on the raw query and attempts to retain the original input as much as possible.

<a name="AlterRetentionPolicyStatement"></a>

## type [AlterRetentionPolicyStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1041-L1065>)

AlterRetentionPolicyStatement represents a command to alter an existing retention policy.

```go
type AlterRetentionPolicyStatement struct {
    // Name of policy to alter.
    Name string

    // Name of the database this policy belongs to.
    Database string

    // Duration data written to this policy will be retained.
    Duration *time.Duration

    // Replication factor for data written to this policy.
    Replication *int

    // Should this policy be set as defalut for the database?
    Default bool

    // Duration of the Shard.
    ShardGroupDuration *time.Duration

    // Cutoff for future writes
    FutureWriteLimit *time.Duration

    // Cutoff for past writes
    PastWriteLimit *time.Duration
}
```


DefaultDatabase returns the default database from the statement.

<a name="CreateContinuousQueryStatement"></a>

## type [CreateContinuousQueryStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L2542-L2557>)

CreateContinuousQueryStatement represents a command for creating a continuous query.

```go
type CreateContinuousQueryStatement struct {
    // Name of the continuous query to be created.
    Name string

    // Name of the database to create the continuous query on.
    Database string

    // Source of data (SELECT statement).
    Source *SelectStatement

    // Interval to resample previous queries.
    ResampleEvery time.Duration

    // Maximum duration to resample previous queries.
    ResampleFor time.Duration
}
```

<a name="CreateDatabaseStatement"></a>

## type [CreateDatabaseStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L588-L610>)

CreateDatabaseStatement represents a command for creating a new database.

```go
type CreateDatabaseStatement struct {
    // Name of the database to be created.
    Name string

    // RetentionPolicyCreate indicates whether the user explicitly wants to create a retention policy.
    RetentionPolicyCreate bool

    // RetentionPolicyDuration indicates retention duration for the new database.
    RetentionPolicyDuration *time.Duration

    // RetentionPolicyReplication indicates retention replication for the new database.
    RetentionPolicyReplication *int

    // RetentionPolicyName indicates retention name for the new database.
    RetentionPolicyName string

    // RetentionPolicyShardGroupDuration indicates shard group duration for the new database.
    RetentionPolicyShardGroupDuration time.Duration

    FutureWriteLimit *time.Duration

    PastWriteLimit *time.Duration
}
```


```go

```

<a name="CreateRetentionPolicyStatement"></a>

## type [CreateRetentionPolicyStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L972-L996>)

CreateRetentionPolicyStatement represents a command to create a retention policy.

```go
type CreateRetentionPolicyStatement struct {
    // Name of policy to create.
    Name string

    // Name of database this policy belongs to.
    Database string

    // Duration data written to this policy will be retained.
    Duration time.Duration

    // Replication factor for data written to this policy.
    Replication int

    // Should this policy be set as default for the database?
    Default bool

    // Shard Duration.
    ShardGroupDuration time.Duration

    // Error on writes after this duration from now
    FutureWriteLimit time.Duration

    // Error on writes before this duration from now
    PastWriteLimit time.Duration
}
```

<a name="DataType"></a>

## type [DataType](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L20>)

DataType represents the primitive data types available in InfluxQL.

```go
type DataType int
```

<a name="Unknown"></a>

```go
const (
    // Unknown primitive data type.
    Unknown DataType = 0
    // Float means the data type is a float.
    Float DataType = 1
    // Integer means the data type is an integer.
    Integer DataType = 2
    // String means the data type is a string of text.
    String DataType = 3
    // Boolean means the data type is a boolean.
    Boolean DataType = 4
    // Time means the data type is a time.
    Time DataType = 5
    // Duration means the data type is a duration of time.
    Duration DataType = 6
    // Tag means the data type is a tag.
    Tag DataType = 7
    // AnyField means the data type is any field.
    AnyField DataType = 8
    // Unsigned means the data type is an unsigned integer.
    Unsigned DataType = 9
)
```

<a name="DataTypeFromString"></a>

### func [DataTypeFromString](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L99>)

```go
func DataTypeFromString(s string) DataType
```

DataTypeFromString returns a data type given the string representation of that data type.

<a name="EvalType"></a>

### func [EvalType](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5084>)

```go
func EvalType(expr Expr, sources Sources, typmap TypeMapper) DataType
```

EvalType evaluates the expression's type.

<a name="InspectDataType"></a>

### func [InspectDataType](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L76>)

```go
func InspectDataType(v interface{}) DataType
```

InspectDataType returns the data type of a given value.

<a name="DataType.LessThan"></a>

### func \(DataType\) [LessThan](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L130>)

```go
func (d DataType) LessThan(other DataType) bool
```

LessThan returns true if the other DataType has greater precedence than the current data type. Unknown has the lowest precedence.

NOTE: This is not the same as using the \`\<\` or \`\>\` operator because the integers used decrease with higher precedence, but Unknown is the lowest precedence at the zero value.

<a name="DataType.String"></a>

### func \(DataType\) [String](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L175>)

```go
func (d DataType) String() string
```

String returns the human\-readable string representation of the DataType.

<a name="DataType.Zero"></a>

### func \(DataType\) [Zero](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L154>)

```go
func (d DataType) Zero() interface{}
```

Zero returns the zero value for the DataType. The return value of this method, when sent back to InspectDataType, may not produce the same value.

<a name="Expr"></a>

## type [Expr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L394-L399>)

Expr represents an expression that can be evaluated to a value.

```go
type Expr interface {
    Node
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="CloneExpr"></a>

### func [CloneExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4092>)

```go
func CloneExpr(expr Expr) Expr
```

CloneExpr returns a deep copy of the expression.

<a name="ConjunctionsToExprSlice"></a>

### func [ConjunctionsToExprSlice](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/utils.go#L14>)

```go
func ConjunctionsToExprSlice(expr Expr) []Expr
```

ConjunctionsToExprSlice finds all children of AndOperators that are not themselves AndOperators, and returns them in a slice. If the root node of expr is not an AndOperator, just returns expr.

```
AND
   /   \
  AND   r    =>   {p, q, r}
 /   \
p     q
```

<a name="ExprsToConjunction"></a>

### func [ExprsToConjunction](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/utils.go#L38>)

```go
func ExprsToConjunction(exprs ...Expr) Expr
```

ExprsToConjunction accepts a variable number of expressions and ANDs them together into a single expression.

```
AND
                    /   \
{p, q, r}    =>    AND   r
                  /   \
                 p     q
```

<a name="MustParseExpr"></a>

### func [MustParseExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/parser.go#L62>)

```go
func MustParseExpr(s string) Expr
```

MustParseExpr parses an expression string and returns its AST. Panic on error.

<a name="ParseExpr"></a>

### func [ParseExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/parser.go#L59>)

```go
func ParseExpr(s string) (Expr, error)
```

ParseExpr parses an expression string and returns its AST representation.

<a name="PartitionExpr"></a>

### func [PartitionExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/utils.go#L68>)

```go
func PartitionExpr(expr Expr, partitionFn func(expression Expr) (bool, error)) (passExpr, failExpr Expr, err error)
```

PartitionExpr accepts a predicate expression, separates it into components that have been logically ANDed together, and applies partitionFn to them. Returns two expressions: one AND tree of the expressions for which partitionFn returned true, and an AND tree of expressions for which partitionFn returned false.

Suppose partitonFn returns true for p and r, and false for q:

```
AND           passExpr     failExpr
   /   \
  AND   r    =>     AND           q
 /   \             /   \
p     q           p     r
```

<a name="Reduce"></a>

### func [Reduce](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5139>)

```go
func Reduce(expr Expr, valuer Valuer) Expr
```

Reduce evaluates expr using the available values in valuer. References that don't exist in valuer are ignored.

<a name="RewriteExpr"></a>

### func [RewriteExpr](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4372>)

```go
func RewriteExpr(expr Expr, fn func(Expr) Expr) Expr
```

RewriteExpr recursively invokes the function to replace each expr. Nodes are traversed depth\-first and rewritten from leaf to root.

## type [FieldMapper](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1267-L1271>)

```go
}
```

<a name="FillOption"></a>

## type [FillOption](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1121>)

FillOption represents different options for filling aggregate windows.

```go
type FillOption int
```

<a name="NullFill"></a>

```go
const (
    // NullFill means that empty aggregate windows will just have null values.
    NullFill FillOption = iota
    // NoFill means that empty aggregate windows will be purged from the result.
    NoFill
    // NumberFill means that empty aggregate windows will be filled with a provided number.
    NumberFill
    // PreviousFill means that empty aggregate windows will be filled with whatever the previous aggregate window had.
    PreviousFill
    // LinearFill means that empty aggregate windows will be filled with whatever a linear value between non null windows.
    LinearFill
)
```

<a name="Node"></a>

## type [Node](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L200-L208>)

Node represents a node in the InfluxDB abstract syntax tree.

```go
type Node interface {

    // String will return the stringified version of Node.
    // Any receivers that are nil will cause String() to return an
    // empty string variable ("").
    String() string
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="Rewrite"></a>

### func [Rewrite](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4302>)

```go
func Rewrite(r Rewriter, node Node) Node
```

Rewrite recursively invokes the rewriter to replace each node. Nodes are traversed depth\-first and rewritten from leaf to root.

<a name="RewriteFunc"></a>

### func [RewriteFunc](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4362>)

```go
func RewriteFunc(node Node, fn func(Node) Node) Node
```

RewriteFunc rewrites a node hierarchy.

<a name="Pos"></a>

## type [Pos](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/token.go#L335-L338>)

Pos specifies the line and character position of a token. The Char and Line are both zero\-based indexes.

```go
}
```

<a name="Privilege"></a>

## type [Privilege](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L766>)

Privilege is a type of action a user can be granted the right to use.

```go
type Privilege int
```

<a name="NoPrivileges"></a>

```go
const (
    // NoPrivileges means no privileges required / granted / revoked.
    NoPrivileges Privilege = iota
    // ReadPrivilege means read privilege required / granted / revoked.
    ReadPrivilege
    // WritePrivilege means write privilege required / granted / revoked.
    WritePrivilege
    // AllPrivileges means all privileges required / granted / revoked.
    AllPrivileges
)
```


String returns a string representation of a Privilege.

```go
type Scanner struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
```

```go
func (s *Scanner) Scan() (tok Token, pos Pos, lit string)
```

Scan returns the next token and position from the underlying reader. Also returns the literal text read for strings, numbers, and duration tokens since these token types can have different literal representations.


ScanRegex consumes a token to find escapes

<a name="SelectStatement"></a>

## type [SelectStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1137-L1197>)

SelectStatement represents a command for extracting data from the database.

```go
type SelectStatement struct {
    // Expressions returned from the selection.
    Fields Fields

    // Target (destination) for the result of a SELECT INTO query.
    Target *Target

    // Expressions used for grouping the selection.
    Dimensions Dimensions

    // Data sources (measurements) that fields are extracted from.
    Sources Sources

    // An expression evaluated on data point.
    Condition Expr

    // Fields to sort results by.
    SortFields SortFields

    // Maximum number of rows to be returned. Unlimited if zero.
    Limit int

    // Returns rows starting at an offset from the first row.
    Offset int

    // Maxiumum number of series to be returned. Unlimited if zero.
    SLimit int

    // Returns series starting at an offset from the first one.
    SOffset int

    // Whether it's a query for raw data values (i.e. not an aggregate).
    IsRawQuery bool

    // What fill option the select statement uses, if any.
    Fill FillOption

    // The value to fill empty aggregate buckets with, if any.
    FillValue interface{}

    // The timezone for the query, if any.
    Location *time.Location

    // Renames the implicit time field name.
    TimeAlias string

    // Removes the "time" column from the output.
    OmitTime bool

    // Removes measurement name from resulting query. Useful for meta queries.
    StripName bool

    // Overrides the output measurement name.
    EmitName string

    // Removes duplicate rows from raw queries.
    Dedupe bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="SelectStatement.Clone"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [Clone](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1213>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) Clone() *SelectStatement
```

Clone returns a deep copy of the statement.

<a name="SelectStatement.ColumnNames"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [ColumnNames](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1743>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) ColumnNames() []string
```

ColumnNames will walk all fields and functions and return the appropriate field names for the select statement while maintaining order of the field names.

<a name="SelectStatement.FieldExprByName"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [FieldExprByName](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1817>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) FieldExprByName(name string) (int, Expr)
```

FieldExprByName returns the expression that matches the field name and the index where this was found. If the name matches one of the arguments to "top" or "bottom", the variable reference inside of the function is returned and the index is of the function call rather than the variable reference. If no expression is found, \-1 is returned for the index and the expression will be nil.

<a name="SelectStatement.GroupByInterval"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [GroupByInterval](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1958>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) GroupByInterval() (time.Duration, error)
```

GroupByInterval extracts the time interval, if specified.

<a name="SelectStatement.GroupByOffset"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [GroupByOffset](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1989>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) GroupByOffset() (time.Duration, error)
```

GroupByOffset extracts the time interval offset, if specified.

<a name="SelectStatement.HasDimensionWildcard"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [HasDimensionWildcard](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1946>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) HasDimensionWildcard() bool
```

HasDimensionWildcard returns whether or not the select statement has at least 1 wildcard in the dimensions aka \`GROUP BY\`.

<a name="SelectStatement.HasFieldWildcard"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [HasFieldWildcard](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1931>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) HasFieldWildcard() (hasWildcard bool)
```

HasFieldWildcard returns whether or not the select statement has at least 1 wildcard in the fields.

<a name="SelectStatement.HasWildcard"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [HasWildcard](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1926>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) HasWildcard() bool
```

HasWildcard returns whether or not the select statement has at least 1 wildcard.

<a name="SelectStatement.Reduce"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [Reduce](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1834>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) Reduce(valuer Valuer) *SelectStatement
```

Reduce calls the Reduce function on the different components of the SelectStatement to reduce the statement.

<a name="SelectStatement.RequiredPrivileges"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [RequiredPrivileges](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1913>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) RequiredPrivileges() (ExecutionPrivileges, error)
```

RequiredPrivileges returns the privilege required to execute the SelectStatement. NOTE: Statement should be normalized first \(database name\(s\) in Sources and Target should be populated\). If the statement has not been normalized, an empty string will be returned for the database name and it is up to the caller to interpret that as the default database.

<a name="SelectStatement.RewriteDistinct"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [RewriteDistinct](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1710>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) RewriteDistinct()
```

RewriteDistinct rewrites the expression to be a call for map/reduce to work correctly. This method assumes all validation has passed.

<a name="SelectStatement.RewriteFields"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [RewriteFields](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1277>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) RewriteFields(m FieldMapper) (*SelectStatement, error)
```

RewriteFields returns the re\-written form of the select statement. Any wildcard query fields are replaced with the supplied fields, and any wildcard GROUP BY fields are replaced with the supplied dimensions. Any fields with no type specifier are rewritten with the appropriate type.

<a name="SelectStatement.RewriteRegexConditions"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [RewriteRegexConditions](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1506>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) RewriteRegexConditions()
```

RewriteRegexConditions rewrites regex conditions to make better use of the database index.

Conditions that can currently be simplified are:

- host =\~ /^foo$/ becomes host = 'foo'
- host \!\~ /^foo$/ becomes host \!= 'foo'

Note: if the regex contains groups, character classes, repetition or similar, it's likely it won't be rewritten. In order to support rewriting regexes with these characters would be a lot more work.

<a name="SelectStatement.RewriteTimeFields"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [RewriteTimeFields](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1729>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) RewriteTimeFields()
```

RewriteTimeFields removes any "time" field references.

<a name="SelectStatement.SetTimeRange"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [SetTimeRange](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L2020>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) SetTimeRange(start, end time.Time) error
```

SetTimeRange sets the start and end time of the select statement to \[start, end\). i.e. start inclusive, end exclusive. This is used commonly for continuous queries so the start and end are in buckets.

<a name="SelectStatement.String"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [String](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1851>)

```go
func (s *SelectStatement) String() string
```

String returns a string representation of the select statement.

<a name="SelectStatement.TimeAscending"></a>

### func \(\*SelectStatement\) [TimeAscending](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L1200>)

<a name="ShowTagKeysStatement"></a>

## type [ShowTagKeysStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L3034-L3065>)

ShowTagKeysStatement represents a command for listing tag keys.

```go
type ShowTagKeysStatement struct {
    // Database to query. If blank, use the default database.
    // The database can also be specified per source in the Sources.
    Database string

    // Data sources that fields are extracted from.
    Sources Sources

    // Op to compare tag keys with
    TagKeyOp Token

    // Literal to compare tag keys with
    TagKeyExpr Expr

    // An expression evaluated on data point.
    Condition Expr

    // Fields to sort results by.
    SortFields SortFields

    // Maximum number of tag keys per measurement. Unlimited if zero.
    Limit int

    // Returns tag keys starting at an offset from the first row.
    Offset int

    // Maxiumum number of series to be returned. Unlimited if zero.
    SLimit int

    // Returns series starting at an offset from the first one.
    SOffset int
}
```

<a name="ShowTagValuesStatement"></a>

## type [ShowTagValuesStatement](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L3179-L3216>)

ShowTagValuesStatement represents a command for listing tag values.

```go
type ShowTagValuesStatement struct {
    // Database to query. If blank, use the default database.
    // The database can also be specified per source in the Sources.
    Database string

    // Data source that fields are extracted from.
    Sources Sources

    // Operation to use when selecting tag key(s).
    // This is one of: (associated TagKeyExpr Literal
    // type in parentheses:
    //  EQ (*StringLiteral)
    // NEQ (*StringLiteral)
    // IN (*ListLiteral)
    // EQREGEX (*RegexLiteral)
    // NEQREGEX (*RegexLiteral)
    Op  Token

    // Literal to compare the tag key(s) with.
    // The value of the above Op field determines its dynamic type.
    TagKeyExpr Literal

    // An expression evaluated on each data point.
    Condition Expr

    // NOTE: The specfication doesn't permit ORDER BY in
    // a SHOW TAG VALUES statement and this field is
    // ignored in practice. It's only left here to maintain
    // backward compatibility.
    SortFields SortFields

    // Maximum number of rows to be returned.
    // Unlimited if zero.
    Limit int

    // Returns rows starting at an offset from the first row.
    Offset int
}
```


<a name="Token"></a>


<a name="ILLEGAL"></a>These are a comprehensive list of InfluxQL language tokens.

```go
const (
    // ILLEGAL Token, EOF, WS are Special InfluxQL tokens.
    ILLEGAL Token = iota
    EOF
    WS
    COMMENT

    // IDENT and the following are InfluxQL literal tokens.
    IDENT       // main
    BOUNDPARAM  // $param
    NUMBER      // 12345.67
    INTEGER     // 12345
    DURATIONVAL // 13h
    STRING      // "abc"
    BADSTRING   // "abc
    BADESCAPE   // \q
    TRUE        // true
    FALSE       // false
    REGEX       // Regular expressions
    BADREGEX    // `.*

    // ADD and the following are InfluxQL Operators
    ADD         // +
    SUB         // -
    MUL         // *
    DIV         // /
    MOD         // %
    BITWISE_AND // &
    BITWISE_OR  // |
    BITWISE_XOR // ^

    AND // AND
    OR  // OR

    EQ       // =
    NEQ      // !=
    EQREGEX  // =~
    NEQREGEX // !~
    LT       // <
    LTE      // <=
    GT       // >
    GTE      // >=

    LPAREN      // (
    RPAREN      // )
    COMMA       // ,
    COLON       // :
    DOUBLECOLON // ::
    SEMICOLON   // ;
    DOT         // .

    // ALL and the following are InfluxQL Keywords
    ALL
    ALTER
    ANALYZE
    ANY
    AS
    ASC
    BEGIN
    BY
    CARDINALITY
    CREATE
    CONTINUOUS
    DATABASE
    DATABASES
    DEFAULT
    DELETE
    DESC
    DESTINATIONS
    DIAGNOSTICS
    DISTINCT
    DROP
    DURATION
    END
    EVERY
    EXACT
    EXPLAIN
    FIELD
    FOR
    FROM
    FUTURE
    GRANT
    GRANTS
    GROUP
    GROUPS
    IN
    INF
    INSERT
    INTO
    KEY
    KEYS
    KILL
    LIMIT
    MEASUREMENT
    MEASUREMENTS
    NAME
    OFFSET
    ON
    ORDER
    PASSWORD
    PAST
    POLICY
    POLICIES
    PRIVILEGES
    QUERIES
    QUERY
    READ
    REPLICATION
    RESAMPLE
    RETENTION
    REVOKE
    SELECT
    SERIES
    SET
    SHOW
    SHARD
    SHARDS
    SLIMIT
    SOFFSET
    STATS
    SUBSCRIPTION
    SUBSCRIPTIONS
    TAG
    TO
    USER
    USERS
    VALUES
    VERBOSE
    WHERE
    WITH
    WRITE
)
```

<a name="TypeError"></a>

## type [TypeError](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5072-L5077>)

TypeError is an error when two types are incompatible.

```go
type TypeError struct {
    // Expr contains the expression that generated the type error.
    Expr Expr
    // Message contains the informational message about the type error.
    Message string
}
```

<a name="TypeError.Error"></a>

### func \(\*TypeError\) [Error](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5079>)

```go
func (e *TypeError) Error() string
```

<a name="TypeMapper"></a>

## type [TypeMapper](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4856-L4858>)

TypeMapper maps a data type to the measurement and field.

```go
type TypeMapper interface {
    MapType(measurement *Measurement, field string) DataType
}
```

<a name="MultiTypeMapper"></a>

### func [MultiTypeMapper](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4876>)

```go
func MultiTypeMapper(mappers ...TypeMapper) TypeMapper
```

MultiTypeMapper combines multiple TypeMappers into a single one. The MultiTypeMapper will return the first type that is not Unknown. It will not iterate through all of them to find the highest priority one.

<a name="TypeValuerEval"></a>

## type [TypeValuerEval](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4905-L4908>)

TypeValuerEval evaluates an expression to determine its output type.

```go
type TypeValuerEval struct {
    TypeMapper TypeMapper
    Sources    Sources
}
```

<a name="TypeValuerEval.EvalType"></a>

### func \(\*TypeValuerEval\) [EvalType](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4915>)

```go
func (v *TypeValuerEval) EvalType(expr Expr) (DataType, error)
```

EvalType returns the type for an expression. If the expression cannot be evaluated for some reason, like incompatible types, it is returned as a TypeError in the error. If the error is non\-fatal so we can continue even though an error happened, true will be returned. This function assumes that the expression has already been reduced.

<a name="UnsignedLiteral"></a>

## type [UnsignedLiteral](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L3830-L3832>)

UnsignedLiteral represents an unsigned literal. The parser will only use an unsigned literal if the parsed integer is greater than math.MaxInt64.

```go
type UnsignedLiteral struct {
    Val uint64
}
```

<a name="UnsignedLiteral.String"></a>

### func \(\*UnsignedLiteral\) [String](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L3835>)

```go
func (l *UnsignedLiteral) String() string
```

String returns a string representation of the literal.

<a name="Value"></a>

## type [Value](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/params.go#L13-L16>)

Value represents a value that can be bound to a parameter when parsing the query.

```go
type Value interface {
    TokenType() Token
    Value() string
}
```

<a name="BindValue"></a>

### func [BindValue](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/params.go#L47>)

```go
func BindValue(v interface{}) Value
```

BindValue will bind an interface value to its influxql value. This method of binding values only supports literals.

<a name="Valuer"></a>

## type [Valuer](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5776-L5779>)

Valuer is the interface that wraps the Value\(\) method.

```go
type Valuer interface {
    // Value returns the value and existence flag for a given key.
    Value(key string) (interface{}, bool)
}
```

<a name="MultiValuer"></a>

### func [MultiValuer](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5833>)

```go
func MultiValuer(valuers ...Valuer) Valuer
```

MultiValuer returns a Valuer that iterates over multiple Valuer instances to find a match.

<a name="ValuerEval"></a>

## type [ValuerEval](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4416-L4422>)

ValuerEval will evaluate an expression using the Valuer.

```go
type ValuerEval struct {
    Valuer Valuer

    // IntegerFloatDivision will set the eval system to treat
    // a division between two integers as a floating point division.
    IntegerFloatDivision bool
}
```

<a name="ValuerEval.Eval"></a>

### func \(\*ValuerEval\) [Eval](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4425>)

```go
func (v *ValuerEval) Eval(expr Expr) interface{}
```

Eval evaluates an expression and returns a value.

<a name="ValuerEval.EvalBool"></a>

### func \(\*ValuerEval\) [EvalBool](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4470>)

```go
func (v *ValuerEval) EvalBool(expr Expr) bool
```

EvalBool evaluates expr and returns true if result is a boolean true. Otherwise returns false.

<a name="Visitor"></a>

## type [Visitor](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L4154-L4156>)

Visitor can be called by Walk to traverse an AST hierarchy. The Visit\(\) function is called once per node.

```go
type Visitor interface {

}
```

<a name="ZoneValuer"></a>

## type [ZoneValuer](<https://github.com/influxdata/influxql/blob/master/ast.go#L5790-L5796>)

ZoneValuer is the interface that specifies the current time zone.

```go
type ZoneValuer interface {
    Valuer

    // Zone returns the time zone location. This function may return nil
    // if no time zone is known.
    Zone() *time.Location
}
```
